svg.title: Data Platform — Federated Producer Journey (data stays in producer's GCP project)
svg.text: Data stays in the producer's own GCP project. The platform registers the semantic model, brokers access, and never copies the data.

g.text: no ingestion config — no topic declared

svg.text: PHASE 2 — REVIEW, CI, CD (same pipeline as standard path)
PHASE 3 — SAGA APPLIES federated-contract module (no Kafka topic, no Dataflow job)

g.text: no topic · no schema reg · no connector

svg.text: PRODUCER'S OWN GCP PROJECT (data never moves — platform only brokers access)
platform never reads or copies it

g.text: platform SA must be granted this

svg.text: PHASE 4 — CONSUMER ACCESS (approval triggers cross-project provisioning)

g.text: New Kafka topic / Pub/Sub / REST / GQL
data IS copied, per consumer grant only
All access grants recorded to Knowledge Catalog
direct IAM, Kafka, Pub/Sub, REST & GraphQL — all recorded so "who has access" is always answerable
BigQuery / Cloud SQL in producer project

svg.text: Revocation: terraform-apply-job tears down whatever was created — the cross-project IAM binding, the fanout Dataflow job, the GraphQL endpoint, and the Knowledge Catalog access record — same mechanism as any other grant.
gray = external/git · blue = Cloud Run · teal = GCP-managed / IAM grant · amber = new resource per grant
Federated path: no Kafka topic · no Dataflow ingestion job · no managed connector · data never enters platform storage
